transcript.text: If the school photoshoot turns into a 'who wore the most awkward hearing aid' contest, I'm going to win. My ears feel like they're on a break, and honestly, the whole thing feels like a circus of my own insecurities. I just want to blend in, but I don't know if I even fit in anywhere.

It can be really overwhelming to feel like you're the only one who's struggling to fit in, especially when something like a school photoshoot comes up and makes you feel even more self-conscious. It sounds like your hearing aid is just one part of a bigger feeling of not being like everyone else. Yeah, that can be a really tough spot to be in. What's been the hardest part of dealing with these feelings for you?
I guess the hardest part is that everyone seems to be judging my ears and my looks, like I'm always on display.
Whatever you want to say next, I'm listening.
I feel like I'm about to get judged for my ears again. The temporary hearing aids look like a weird accessory and I just want to blend in. I feel so alone.

You're feeling like you're under a microscope, and it's exhausting trying to hide or downplay your hearing aids. It's like you're constantly waiting for someone to notice and judge you. Yeah, that can be a really isolating experience. It sounds like you're carrying this weight of feeling like you don't fit in, and it's affecting how you see yourself.

I keep thinking everyone is staring at my ears, like I'm a walking billboard. It's exhausting, and I just want to disappear into the background. I feel so isolated, like no one actually sees me beyond the hearing aids.

Your hearing aids have become a focal point of your anxiety, making everyday situations feel like a source of potential judgment. It's not just about the hearing aids themselves, but about the fear of being seen as different and the exhaustion that comes with trying to blend in. You've mentioned feeling like you're a walking billboard, and that's a really tough feeling to navigate. Can you tell me more about what you think would help you feel less like you're on display?

I guess I’d just want something that looks cool, not like a medical device—maybe a band that doubles as a photo prop?  But honestly, I just want people to notice my art, not my ears, and I feel like no one’s really looking past the temporary thing.

You're looking for a way to express yourself that doesn't draw attention to your hearing aids, and you wish people could see beyond them to your art. That makes sense, given how much you value your creativity. Have you considered talking to an audiologist about customizing your hearing aids to better fit your personal style?

I don't know if an audiologist can make them look cool, but I'd love a band that looks like a prop. I just want people to see my art, not my medical gear, and I'm scared they'll judge me again. Maybe I should just keep it low-key.

You're really worried that your hearing aids will be the first thing people notice, and that's a heavy burden to carry. It's like you're feeling invisible as a person, and your art is what you wish people could see instead. Yeah, it makes sense that you'd want something that makes you feel more confident, like a band that looks like a prop. What do you think it would take for you to feel like your art is being seen, rather than your hearing aids?

I could paint my hearing aids like a comic book hero—maybe a neon cape that screams 'art' instead of 'medical device'. But honestly, I'm still terrified they'll stare even if I do that. Maybe if I just wear a giant smiley face on them, people will notice my art instead.

You're really creative, and I love the idea of painting your hearing aids like a comic book hero - that neon cape idea is awesome. It sounds like you're trying to find a way to express yourself and showcase your art, rather than letting your hearing aids define you. What do you think it would take for you to feel like your art is being seen, rather than your hearing aids, and how can you use that creativity to boost your confidence?

I guess if I paint them with a neon cape, maybe they'll see a comic hero instead of a medical device and stop staring—though I still feel like everyone’s judging my ears like a fashion statement.  I just want people to notice my art, not the temporary thing on my head.  It’s hard to feel seen when I feel so alone in this crowd.

You're really struggling to feel seen as more than your hearing aids, and it's like you're trying to find a way to break free from that feeling of being judged all the time. Painting your hearing aids with a neon cape is a pretty creative way to take control of how you're perceived, and it sounds like you're hoping that'll help people notice your art instead. Yeah, it can be really tough to feel like you're being seen as an individual when you feel like your hearing aids are defining you. What do you think is the most important thing for you to express through your art, and how can you use that to start feeling more confident in yourself?

I guess I'll just paint my aids like a comic hero, but I'm still terrified they'll think I'm doing a weird cosplay. The idea of being seen for my art feels far from reality when everyone focuses on my ears. Honestly, I just want someone to notice the pictures I draw, not the gadgets on my head.

It's really tough to feel like your art is being ignored when you feel like your hearing aids are the center of attention. You've been trying to find ways to express yourself and showcase your creativity, like painting your hearing aids with a neon cape, but the fear of being judged is still there. It sounds like you're feeling a bit trapped between wanting to express yourself and worrying about what others will think. What's the most important thing you want people to see when they look at you - is it your art, your personality, or something else entirely?

I just want them to see the kid who draws comics, not the ears that are on display. My art feels like a secret, but I hope it can be the thing that makes them look past the hearing aids. I keep worrying that they'll still focus on my medical gear instead of my pictures.

You're feeling like your art is a secret, and you wish people could see the kid who draws comics, not just the hearing aids. It's like you're trying to find a way to break free from the feeling of being defined by your medical gear, and instead, be recognized for your creativity. That takes a lot of courage, and I can see why you'd want to express yourself in a way that showcases your art, not your hearing aids. Your comics are a part of who you are, and it's understandable that you'd want people to see that side of you.

turn_traces.user_message: If the school photoshoot turns into a 'who wore the most awkward hearing aid' contest, I'm going to win. My ears feel like they're on a break, and honestly, the whole thing feels like a circus of my own insecurities. I just want to blend in, but I don't know if I even fit in anywhere.
